Anyway, I hope you have fun!

reply_text: @secondsquirrel1168 You're right: it was written by René Goscinny and illustrated by Albert Uderzo. Both were from france. They met in Brussels (Belgium) and joined eachother for work. They worked together at Journal TinTin, which was published by Lombard (Belgian): the same publisher for their comic books (and the French publisher Dargaud). So it's French indeed, but mostly published by a Belgian publisher :)

reply_text: Yes, agree! Not always easy to do for the references. The « boot » that César mention is a reference to « la botte Italienne » (Italy being the shape of a boot, very common in french, not sure for English). And the two spectators at the end, english version says « Only in Armorica »  while in french the version says « Oh, that’s the Armoricans » Two subtle way to play with words!
